# americancloud_vm (Resource)

A virtual machine. Provisions asynchronously — `apply` blocks until the VM is running. `vcpu`, `memory_mb`, and `root_disk_gb` change in place (the platform reboots the VM to apply); name, image, network, and region are immutable (changing them replaces the VM).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "americancloud_isolated_network" "example" {
  name   = "vm-net"
  region = "us-west-0"
}

resource "americancloud_ssh_key" "example" {
  name       = "deploy-key"
  public_key = file("~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub")
}

# VM on a Terraform-managed network.
resource "americancloud_vm" "example" {
  name                = "web-1"
  region              = "us-west-0"
  vm_package          = "standard-custom" # from list_vm_packages
  vcpu                = 1
  memory_mb           = 2048
  root_disk_gb        = 25
  image               = "ubuntu-24.04" # from list_images
  network             = americancloud_isolated_network.example.id
  subscription_period = "hourly"

  keypairs  = [americancloud_ssh_key.example.name]
  user_data = <<-EOT
    #cloud-config
    packages: [nginx]
  EOT
  tags      = ["web", "production"]
}

# VM with platform-managed network access: omit `network` and the platform
# auto-creates an isolated network, opening the requested inbound ports
# (port forwarding + firewall on the network's public IP).
resource "americancloud_vm" "reachable" {
  name                = "web-2"
  region              = "us-west-0"
  vm_package          = "standard-custom"
  vcpu                = 1
  memory_mb           = 2048
  root_disk_gb        = 25
  image               = "ubuntu-24.04"
  subscription_period = "hourly"

  keypairs = [americancloud_ssh_key.example.name]

  network_access = {
    allow_egress_all = true
    inbound_ports = [
      { port = 22, protocol = "TCP" },
      { port = 443, protocol = "TCP" },
    ]
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `image` (String) Image label (from `list_images`). Forces replacement.
- `memory_mb` (Number) Memory in MB. Changing it resizes the VM in place (reboots it).
- `name` (String) VM name. Forces replacement.
- `region` (String) Region label, e.g. `us-west-0`. Forces replacement.
- `root_disk_gb` (Number) Root disk size in GB (min 25). Can be grown in place (reboots the VM); shrinking is rejected.
- `subscription_period` (String) Billing period: `hourly` or `monthly`. Forces replacement.
- `vcpu` (Number) Number of vCPUs. Changing it resizes the VM in place (reboots it).
- `vm_package` (String) VM package label (from `list_vm_packages`). Forces replacement.

### Optional

- `keypairs` (Set of String) SSH key names to install (see `americancloud_ssh_key`). Keys are installed for the image's access user — `root` on the stock Ubuntu images. Forces replacement. Not recoverable by `terraform import`.
- `network` (String) Network UUID to attach the VM to. Omit to have the platform auto-create an isolated network (required for `network_access`; the auto-created network is not managed by Terraform and survives the VM). Forces replacement.
- `network_access` (Attributes) Create-time network access for a platform-created network: opens the requested inbound ports (port forwarding + firewall rules on the network's public IP) and optionally allows all egress. Only honored when `network` is omitted — conflicts with it. Forces replacement. Not recoverable by `terraform import`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--network_access))
- `tags` (Set of String) Tags to assign to the VM. Forces replacement. Not recoverable by `terraform import`.
- `timeouts` (Block, Optional) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--timeouts))
- `user_data` (String) Cloud-init user data executed on first boot, as plain text (the provider base64-encodes it for the API). Forces replacement. Not recoverable by `terraform import`.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Creation time (RFC 3339).
- `id` (String) VM identifier (UUID).
- `ip_address` (String) Primary IP address.
- `root_volume_id` (String) Root volume identifier.
- `status` (String) Current lifecycle status.

<a id="nestedatt--network_access"></a>
### Nested Schema for `network_access`

Required:

- `allow_egress_all` (Boolean) Allow all outbound traffic from the VM.

Optional:

- `inbound_ports` (Attributes List) Inbound ports to open to the VM. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--network_access--inbound_ports))
- `source_cidr` (String) Source CIDR the inbound ports are opened to. Omit for any source.

<a id="nestedatt--network_access--inbound_ports"></a>
### Nested Schema for `network_access.inbound_ports`

Required:

- `port` (Number) Port number.
- `protocol` (String) `TCP` or `UDP`.



<a id="nestedblock--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.
